calendar.matters:
Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Provision And For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance; And For Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Feb-24-2020 Off Calendar

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Provision And For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance; And For Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Nov-25-2019 Continued To Feb-24-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Frederick Crombie appeared by telephone for Barbara Platt.
Adrian Sawyer for Ross Anderson (Respondent).

Mr. Sawyer stated that they have not had mediation, that he believes the matter will not be ripe for mediation until they get closer to trial, that they are rolling into depositions now, that the deadline of completion of mediation is the end of March and suggested setting a two-week trial on April 27th. Counsel is to submit a status statement by February 14, 2020 regarding mediation, the claims that are to be tried, any disputed facts on the claims, a witness list with time estimates and trial readiness. The Petition to Determine Validity of Trust Provision, etc. is continued to February 24, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Provision And For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance; And For Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Oct-28-2019 Continued To Nov-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Provision And For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance; And For Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Aug-12-2019 Continued To Oct-28-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Frederick C. Crombie for Barbara Platt. Adrian Sawyer for Ross Anderson (Respondent).

The Court stated before it today is the Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Provision And For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance; And For Elder Abuse and the amendment to that petition. Mr. Crombie stated that the parties have exchanged written discovery, that they are on the first and second wave of depositions, and that there may be additional depositions that need to taken once the first and second waves are completed. Mr. Sawyer stated that they have made a lot of progress and that the petitioner and respondent's depositions are set in August and September. Mr. Sawyer also stated that most of the deponents are third parties and that they are hoping to identify any "clean up" discovery. Brief discussions are held regarding calendaring. Both counsel indicate that they are set to start trials. The Court stated that it is good that counsel are done with their first round of discovery and that it needs to get the matter in shape/get it ready for trial before it is sent out to Department 206. Mr. Sawyer suggested that the Court have the parties return in October and that they can submit written statements to the Court. The Court noted that it will not go too far into 2020 after the "clean up". The Court then continued the Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Provision And For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance; And For Elder Abuse to October 28, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that counsels' statement(s) should set forth deadlines & proposed trial dates and that counsel are urged to meet and confer regarding a trial plan.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Provision And For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance; And For Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Jun-24-2019 Continued To Aug-12-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Provision And For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance; And For Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Mar-25-2019 Continued To Jun-24-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Fredrick Crombie for Barbara Platt (Petitioner).
Adrian Sawyer for Ross Anderson (Respondent).

Mr. Sawyer stated that he and Mr. Crombie talked, that they are going get into taking depositions, that they will be doing additional discovery and requested coming back in
90-days. The Petition to Determine Validity of Trust Provision, etc. is continued to June 24, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Mar-11-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Frederick Crombie for Barbara Platt (Petitioner). Adrian Sawyer for Ellis Anderson (Respondent).

The Court stated that this matter comes on calendar today for a motion for summary adjudication. Mr. Crombie presented his oral argument in support of his objections to the court's tentative ruling to deny the motion. Mr. Sawyer presented his rebuttal argument.

After hearing oral argument and reviewing the court filed documents, the Court takes the motion for summary adjudication under submission.

Judge John K. Stewart, Clerk: N. Wong, Bailiff: Deputy Chan. Court Reporter: Sheila Pham. CSR:13293. Telephone: 415-300-0469. Email: info@aputscr.com.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Provision And For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance; And For Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Dec-03-2018 Continued To Mar-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Frederick Crombie for Barbara Platt (Petitioner).
Adrian Sawyer for Ross Anderson (Respondent).

Mr. Crombie stated that they both have exchanged discovery, that the respondent has provided some documents, that his Client will be making production of documents today, that they will be filing a motion for summary judgment ("MSJ") and that they have agreed to a hearing date on the MSJ for March 6th. The Court Clerk explained that the requested hearing date is currently available, but a date will not provide until the pleadings are prepared and ready to be filed. Counsel agreed to do private mediation. The Petition to Determine Validity of Trust Provision, etc. is continued to March 25, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: F. Ruiz. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Provision And For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance; And For Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Nov-05-2018 Continued To Nov-19-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Provision And For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance; And For Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Aug-06-2018 Continued To Nov-05-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Frederick Crombie for Barbara Platt (Petitioner).
Adrian Sawyer for Respondent.

Counsel reported that they have conducted some discovery, that they have received responses and documents, and that they are working on some issues regarding Mr. Sawyer's requests. Mr. Sawyer stated that it is a bit early for mediation, that Mr. Crombie will be filing a motion, that more discovery is needed and requested a 90-day continuance. The Court stated that the proper authority regarding venue is Probate Code 17003 and that this Court has jurisdiction. The Petition to Determine Validity of Trust Provision, etc. is continued to November 5, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Provision And For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance; And For Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Jul-02-2018 Continued To Aug-06-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Fredrick Crombie for Barbara Platt (Petitioner)
Adrian Sawyer for Mr. Anderson (Respondent)

Mr. Crombie stated that his client resides in Incline Village, NV. Mr. Sawyer stated that this is also an elder abuse matter, the jurisdiction is proper in California as Mr. Anderson resides in San Francisco, and the property is in San Francisco. The Petition to Determine Validity of Trust Provision and for Intentional Interference with Expected Inheritance, etc. is Taken Under Submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

*** After Hearing ***

This matter is continued to August 6, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 by the Court for Counsel to submit a sworn affidavit regarding the primary residence of the petitioner/beneficiary/trustee.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Provision And For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance; And For Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Mar-05-2018 Continued To Jul-02-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Frederick Crombie for petitioner Barbara Platt; Adrian Sawyer for Ross Anderson.

Responding to an inquiry from the Court, Mr. Crombie stated that the petitioner's principal address is in Incline Village, Nevada, although she is currently visiting in Alamo, California. He said that under Probate Code section 17005 the principal place of trust administration was where Mr. Anderson's office was located, in San Francisco, California.
Mr. Sawyer agreed that venue was in San Francisco under sections 17005 and 395. He said that some of the claims asserted against Mr. Anderson were not probate claims, and therefor venue lies in San Francisco where Mr. Anderson resides.
Mr. Crombie stated his concern with a gift to Mr. Anderson made in the Fourth Amendment of Trust. He believes the Certificate of Independent Review was defective as it was made on an obsolete form, and he plans to bring a motion for summary judgment to test its validity. He requested that discovery be limited pending resolution of that issue. He has an additional concern regarding excessive expenditures made during the last years of the decedent's life.
Mr. Sawyer objected to any restriction on discovery at this time, and requested a trial date.
The Court stated that it was too early to restrict discovery or to set a trial date, and that he would only agree to set a future status hearing date. Mr. Crombie requested that the matter be set 120 days out. Mr. Sawyer agreed that 120 days was appropriate. The matter was continued to July 2, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)
